title: Broken Love
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My Spectre around me night and day
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like a wild beast guards my way;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My Emanation far within
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Weeps incessantly for my sin.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½A fathomless and boundless deep,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ There we wander, there we weep;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ On the hungry craving wind
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My Spectre follows thee behind.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He scents thy footsteps in the snow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Wheresoever thou dost go,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Throן¿½ the wintry hail and rain.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When wilt thou return again?

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dost thou not in pride and scorn
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Fill with tempests all my morn,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And with jealousies and fears
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Fill my pleasant nights with tears?

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Seven of my sweet loves thy knife
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Has bereavן¿½d of their life.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Their marble tombs I built with tears,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And with cold and shuddering fears.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Seven more loves weep night and day
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Round the tombs where my loves lay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And seven more loves attend each night
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Around my couch with torches bright.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And seven more loves in my bed
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Crown with wine my mournful head,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pitying and forgiving all
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Thy transgressions great and small.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½When wilt thou return and view
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My loves, and them to life renew?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When wilt thou return and live?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When wilt thou pity as I forgive?ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Oן¿½er my sins thou sit and moan:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hast thou no sins of thy own?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oן¿½er my sins thou sit and weep,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And lull thy own sins fast asleep.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½What transgressions I commit
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Are for thy transgressions fit.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ They thy harlots, thou their slave;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And my bed becomes their grave.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Never, never, I return:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Still for victory I burn.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Living, thee alone Iן¿½ll have;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And when dead Iן¿½ll be thy grave.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Throן¿½ the Heaven and Earth and Hell
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Thou shalt never, quell:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I will fly and thou pursue:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Night and morn the flight renew.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Poor, pale, pitiable form
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That I follow in a storm;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iron tears and groans of lead
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Bind around my aching head.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Till I turn from Female love
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And root up the Infernal Grove,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I shall never worthy be
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To step into Eternity.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And, to end thy cruel mocks,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Annihilate thee on the rocks,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And another form create
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To be subservient to my fate.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Let us agree to give up love,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And root up the Infernal Grove;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then shall we return and see
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The worlds of happy Eternity.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And throughout all Eternity
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I forgive you, you forgive me.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As our dear Redeemer said:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½This the Wine, and this the Bread.ן¿½ן¿½
meaning:
"Broken Love" is a quintessential example of William Blake's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
